reasoning: The 30-day average over a 360-day period represents the mean of roughly 12 periods. Protest events often follow a right-skewed distribution, where sudden spikes in a few months drag the overall average up, leaving the median below the mean. Thus, any randomly selected 30-day period has a less than 50% chance of exceeding the annual average. Additionally, the likely resolution date (late January 2026) covers late December to late January, a period where colder winter weather in Turkey generally depresses protest activity compared to spring and summer months. These factors make it more likely than not that the count will be below the baseline average.
